canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, CommonsenseQA 2.0 is consistently described as a dataset used for evaluation. Its most prevalent application is to measure the construct of 'Commonsense understanding', a use case cited across multiple papers. A less common application, found in a single paper, is its use to measure 'Faithfulness'. The dataset is characterized as containing a 'large number of questions' in a multiple-choice format. One paper further specifies that these questions pertain to both 'entity knowledge and commonsense reasoning' (UniCoTT: A Unified Framework for Structural Chain-of-Thought Distillation). The definitions converge on its role as an instrument for assessing commonsense reasoning capabilities.
papers: NoVo: Norm Voting off Hallucinations with Attention Heads in Large Language Models
OCEAN: Offline Chain-of-thought Evaluation and Alignment in Large Language Models
UniCoTT: A Unified Framework for Structural Chain-of-Thought Distillation

definitions.text: A dataset for commonsense reasoning containing a large number of questions.
definitions.paper: UniCoTT: A Unified Framework for Structural Chain-of-Thought Distillation

definitions.text: A dataset used to evaluate commonsense reasoning capabilities through multiple-choice questions.
